title: The Gap Winner Rule Inside a Prime Gap
description: A static-plan draft for a GWR explainer that shows integer blocks in a prime gap, exact divisor counts on the composite interior, minimum-divisor selection, leftmost tie resolution, raw-Z agreement, and the distinction between the selected interior integer and the next prime endpoint.

scenes.title: A Prime Gap And Its Interior
scenes.description: Introduce two consecutive primes and the composite integers in the interior of the prime gap.
visual_ideas: Use Rectangle mobjects as integer blocks for 23 through 29 on a NumberLine.
Color 23 and 29 as the prime endpoints and bracket 24 through 28 as the gap interior.
Keep the right prime endpoint visually present while the rule compares only the interior composite integers.

scenes.title: Divisor Counts On The Interior
scenes.description: Show exact divisor counts on each interior composite in the prime gap from 23 to 29.
visual_ideas: Transform each integer block label into a two-line label with n and d(n).
Use vertical bar height or brightness to encode divisor count values 8, 3, 4, 4, and 6.
Add a compact table-like overlay only after the block labels are visible.

scenes.title: Minimum Divisor Count
scenes.description: Evaluate the first GWR clause: choose the minimum divisor count among the interior composites.
visual_ideas: Animate a horizontal threshold line down to d(n)=3.
Fade all blocks with divisor count greater than 3.
Highlight 25 as the selected GWR integer for this prime gap.

scenes.title: Leftmost Tie Rule
scenes.description: Use the prime gap from 89 to 97 to show the second GWR clause when multiple interior composites share the minimum divisor count.
visual_ideas: Display blocks 90 through 96 with divisor counts 12, 4, 6, 4, 4, 4, and 12.
Light all d(n)=4 blocks in cyan as tied minimizers.
Move a scan cursor left to right and lock 91 as the leftmost minimizer of d(n).

scenes.title: Raw-Z Winner Collapses Onto GWR
scenes.description: Introduce raw-Z after the mechanical rule is visible, then show that its score winner lands on the same interior integer.
visual_ideas: Draw a smooth score profile over the 24 through 28 blocks.
Place a peak marker above 25 and align it with the existing GWR highlight.
Show the formula Z_raw(n)=n^(1-d(n)/2) as a small caption under the gap interior.

scenes.title: Interior Winner Versus Next Prime
scenes.description: Preserve the mathematical distinction that GWR selects an interior composite integer, not the next prime endpoint.
visual_ideas: Keep the GWR-selected interior integer gold and keep the next prime endpoint as a separate marked block.
Do not draw q=w+1 as a general rule.
End with the prime gap endpoints 23 and 29 visually separate from the selected interior integer 25.

scenes.title: Recap
scenes.description: Restate the rule in one visual pass: measure divisor counts, choose the minimum, break ties leftmost, and keep the selected interior integer distinct from the next prime endpoint.
visual_ideas: Stack four compact icons: prime gap, divisor counts, leftmost minimum, selected interior integer versus next prime endpoint.
Reuse the same colors from the earlier scenes.
Avoid new examples in the recap.
